cross_model.reasoning:
Step 1: Query asks for a party/night-out mini dress in wine/burgundy. Hard constraints: category = dresses, fit = mini, color preference = wine/burgundy, occasion = party/night_out.
Step 2 (Set A - CLIP): Images 1-5 show mostly short/mini party styles — asymmetrical black mini, navy satin mini, dark-brown leather-effect mini, a long black piece (one mismatch), and an aubergine (wine-adjacent) short balloon mini. Metadata matches 'dresses' and several items are explicitly mini; one clear color match (AUBERGINE) and generally party-appropriate styling.
Step 3 (Set B - FashionCLIP): Images 6-10 include the same AUBERGINE short balloon dress, but several other picks are midis/long dresses or darker brown/black items (velvet maxi, lace long dress, midis). Fewer true minis and fewer direct wine/burgundy color matches despite party fabrics.
Step 4: Relevance to hard constraints — CLIP supplies more mini silhouettes and contains an aubergine/wine-adjacent piece; FashionCLIP repeats the aubergine but includes more midi/long lengths and non-wine colors, reducing strict constraint match.
Step 5: Occasion appropriateness — both sets have party-appropriate materials (velvet, lace, leather-effect), but CLIP offers more mini, club-friendly looks matching 'party/night_out'.
Step 6: Visual diversity — FashionCLIP has diverse fabrics and lengths but less useful diversity for the mini/wine requirement. CLIP offers several mini styles (different fabrics/edginess) giving the user meaningful mini options.
Step 7: Overall reflection of user intent — CLIP better reflects the explicit mini + party intent and provides a wine-adjacent color; FashionCLIP is richer in evening fabrics but fails the mini/color constraint more often.
Step 8: Scores assigned: CLIP = 4 (above average — majority suitable and visually appropriate), FashionCLIP = 3 (average — only a minority clearly meet the mini + wine constraint).
Step 9: Winner: clip — CLIP better satisfies the user's mini + party + color intent overall.
